frame_analyses.description: Needle is being loaded onto the needle driver.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Secure grip observed on the needle.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is being passed through the Penrose drain marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alignment is mostly central with slight deviation.

frame_analyses.description: First throw initiated for surgeon's knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw observed, appears firm and secure.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw in progress with hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch correctly executed, single throw used.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being tied with another hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switch and single throw maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Both ends of the suture being cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cuts made close to the knot.

knot_assessments.notes: Surgeon's knot appears firm and well executed.
Single throw appears adequate, hand switch accurate.
Third knot secure with correct hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit closure appears complete and intact.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated a proficient technique with proper hand switching and secure throws, resulting in a strong and stable knot.
improvement_suggestions: Reduce suture deviation from marked points.
Maintain consistent tension throughout the throws to prevent gaps.
